Military


Ukraine Conflict News





25 September 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Coast Guard offloads more than $156 million worth of cocaine in San Diego US Coast Guard 25 Sep 2025 -- The crew of U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Midgett (WMSL 757) offloads approximately 21,126 pounds of cocaine, with an estimated value of more than $156.4 million, Thursday in San Diego.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Lithuanian defense delegation visits Pennsylvania Guard drone facility Army News 25 Sep 2025 -- A delegation of more than 20 Lithuanian defense, diplomatic and business officials visited the Unmanned Aircraft Systems, or UAS, facility Sept. 23, as part of a five-day mission in the United States.
  • National Guard exceeds fiscal year 2025 recruiting goals Army News 25 Sep 2025 -- Amid a historic surge in military recruiting, the National Guard exceeded its fiscal year 2025 goals, reflecting a broader trend of robust enlistment across the U.S. military.
  • Falcon Peak highlights need to counter UAS threat US Northern Command 25 Sep 2025 -- Unmanned aerial systems have transformed warfare much like the invention of gunpowder and tanks.
  • Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star Returns to Seattle after 308 days US Coast Guard 25 Sep 2025 -- After 308 days away from its Seattle home port, the 49-year-old U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) and crew returned home Tuesday.
  • U.S. Navy Decommissions Avenger-class Mine Countermeasures Ships in Bahrain US Navy 25 Sep 2025 -- After nearly 40 years of active service, three recently decommissioned U.S. Navy Avenger-class mine countermeasures ships and their crews were recognized in conjunction with a final decommissioning ceremony for USS Devastator (MCM 6) on board Naval Support Activity (NSA) Bahrain, Sept. 25.

  • Protective Fence 25 to boost Allied Airpower readiness with dispersed operations NATO Allied Air Command 25 Sep 2025 -- From September 22 to 26, 2025, the Finnish Air Force are executing Exercise Protective Fence 25, bringing together around 50 aircraft and 1,200 personnel across Finland and Sweden.

  • Secretary Noem Issues Statement of Support to Create a Secretary of the United States Coast Guard DHS 25 Sep 2025 -- Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem publicly endorsed a letter delivered by Secretary of War Pete Hegseth supporting the creation of a Secretary of the United States Coast Guard.

  • Lithuanian defense delegation visits Pennsylvania Guard drone facility Army News 25 Sep 2025 -- A delegation of more than 20 Lithuanian defense, diplomatic and business officials visited the Unmanned Aircraft Systems, or UAS, facility Sept. 23, as part of a five-day mission in the United States.
  • National Guard exceeds fiscal year 2025 recruiting goals Army News 25 Sep 2025 -- Amid a historic surge in military recruiting, the National Guard exceeded its fiscal year 2025 goals, reflecting a broader trend of robust enlistment across the U.S. military.
  • Falcon Peak highlights need to counter UAS threat US Northern Command 25 Sep 2025 -- Unmanned aerial systems have transformed warfare much like the invention of gunpowder and tanks.
  • Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star Returns to Seattle after 308 days US Coast Guard 25 Sep 2025 -- After 308 days away from its Seattle home port, the 49-year-old U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) and crew returned home Tuesday.
  • U.S. Navy Decommissions Avenger-class Mine Countermeasures Ships in Bahrain US Navy 25 Sep 2025 -- After nearly 40 years of active service, three recently decommissioned U.S. Navy Avenger-class mine countermeasures ships and their crews were recognized in conjunction with a final decommissioning ceremony for USS Devastator (MCM 6) on board Naval Support Activity (NSA) Bahrain, Sept. 25.

  • Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Mark Rutte Discussed Expansion of the PURL Initiative President of Ukraine 25 Sep 2025
  • Government doubles innovation initiative for Ukraine to SEK 300 million Government Offices of Sweden 25 Sep 2025 -- The Government has previously tasked the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration with identifying, testing and funding solutions for defence innovation needed by Ukraine.

  • Commission of aircraft-carrier Fujian drawing near: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 25 Sep 2025 -- "This is a milestone in our development of aircraft carriers. The trials and training of PLANS Fujian are well underway in accordance with our plan. I believe the much-anticipated moment is drawing near," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's defense ministry, at a regular press conference on Thursday.
  • Regular Press Conference of the Ministry of National Defense on September 25, 2025 PRC MND 25 Sep 2025

  • DRDO carries out the successful launch of Intermediate Range Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system India PIB 25 Sep 2025 -- DRDO, in collaboration with the Strategic Forces Command (SFC), has carried out the successful launch of Intermediate Range Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system, under a full operational scenario on 24th Sep 2025
  • MoD signs Rs. 62,370 crore contract with HAL for procurement of 97 LCA Mk1A aircraft for IAF India PIB 25 Sep 2025 -- Ministry of Defence (MoD) signed a contract with Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) for procurement of 97 Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Mk1A, including 68 fighters and 29 twin seaters, along with associated equipment, for Indian Air Force, at a cost of over Rs

  • New Zealand among five nations uniting for Exercise Bersama Lima New Zealand Defence Force 25 Sep 2025 -- Ships, fighter jets and cyber teams are converging in Southeast Asia this month as five nations come together for Exercise Bersama Lima 25, a major training activity under the long-standing Five Power Defence Arrangements (FPDA).

  • Government turns the sod on ground-breaking Defence housing plan New Zealand Defence Force 25 Sep 2025 -- The start of the Linton Homes for Families project has been marked with a sod turning ceremony by Associate Minister of Defence Chris Penk this week.

  • Tsahkna meets with Rubio: NATO Allies are firmly committed to collective defense Estonia MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- On September 24, during the UN High-Level Week, Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss primarily European and U.S. cooperation in pressuring Russia toward peace, as well as the security of NATO allies.

  • EDA, NATO enable real-world testing of unmanned systems European Defence Agency 25 Sep 2025The European Defence Agency (EDA), NATO and the Portuguese Navy co-organised the 15th Robotic Experimentation and Prototyping using Maritime Unmanned Systems (REPMUS), the world's largest event for testing unmanned maritime systems, held off the coast of Portugal.

  • Green light for Army communications upgrade New Zealand Government 25 Sep 2025 -- The New Zealand Army is set to receive a suite of hi-tech digital communications devices and systems to help it protect our country and our people, Defence Minister Judith Collins announced today.

  • Germany - AIM-120D-3 Advanced Medium Range Air-to-Air Missiles DSCA 25 Sep 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Germany of AIM-120D-3 Advanced Medium Range Air-to-Air Missiles and related equipment for an estimated cost of $1.23 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.

  • AFP says reported oust-PBBM plot 'baseless, unfounded' Philippine News Agency 25 Sep 2025 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Thursday dismissed the alleged destabilization plot to oust President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr., calling such information "baseless" and "unfounded."

  • Disarmament over destruction: A renewed push for a world without nuclear weapons UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- Nuclear weapons continue to pose an existential threat to humanity. On Friday in New York as high-level week draws to a close, world leaders will revisit the grave dangers these weapons pose and push for renewed global efforts to eliminate them.

  • Bratislava hosts CEDC + WB Defence Ministers Meeting on regional defence cooperation Ministry of Defence of The Slovak Republic 25 Sep 2025 -- A Slovak Ministry of Defence (SVK MOD) delegation led by Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence Robert Kaliňák gave a warm welcome to the Central European Defence Cooperation (CEDC) and Western Balkans (WB) Defence Ministers and Senior Officials as they met for the CEDC + WB Defence Ministers Meeting at Bratislava Castle on 22-23 September 2025.

  • New Zealand among five nations uniting for Exercise Bersama Lima New Zealand Defence Force 25 Sep 2025 -- Ships, fighter jets and cyber teams are converging in Southeast Asia this month as five nations come together for Exercise Bersama Lima 25, a major training activity under the long-standing Five Power Defence Arrangements (FPDA).
  • Government turns the sod on ground-breaking Defence housing plan New Zealand Defence Force 25 Sep 2025 -- The start of the Linton Homes for Families project has been marked with a sod turning ceremony by Associate Minister of Defence Chris Penk this week.

  • Tsahkna meets with Rubio: NATO Allies are firmly committed to collective defense Estonia MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- On September 24, during the UN High-Level Week, Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss primarily European and U.S. cooperation in pressuring Russia toward peace, as well as the security of NATO allies.

  • Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau at the Panel "The Global Refugee and Asylum System: What Went Wrong and How to Fix It" US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau Leads UNGA Event on Migration Reform US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Determination of a Haitian National as Jeopardizing U.S. Foreign Policy Objectives Regarding Haiti and the Safety of the United States US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Reinforcing Cooperation to Achieve a Secure and Stable Maritime Domain US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025 -- On September 24, Secretary Rubio co-hosted a ministerial meeting on "Reinforcing Cooperation to Achieve a Secure and Stable Maritime Domain," alongside his counterparts from Australia, Estonia, Greece, Japan, the Netherlands, the Philippines, Romania, and the UK, among others.
  • Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau at the Panel "The Global Refugee and Asylum System: What Went Wrong and How to Fix It" US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau Leads UNGA Event on Migration Reform US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Determination of a Haitian National as Jeopardizing U.S. Foreign Policy Objectives Regarding Haiti and the Safety of the United States US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025
  • Reinforcing Cooperation to Achieve a Secure and Stable Maritime Domain US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025 -- On September 24, Secretary Rubio co-hosted a ministerial meeting on "Reinforcing Cooperation to Achieve a Secure and Stable Maritime Domain," alongside his counterparts from Australia, Estonia, Greece, Japan, the Netherlands, the Philippines, Romania, and the UK, among others.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 25 Sep 2025 -- "The shameful capitulation of some leaders to Palestinian terror does not obligate Israel in any way. There will be no Palestinian state."
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at Ben-Gurion International Airport Israel - Prime Minister 25 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu, this morning , at Ben-Gurion International Airport, prior to departing on a diplomatic visit to the US, where he will address the UN General Assembly and meet with US President Donald Trump:

  • Press Briefing by IDF Spokesperson BG Effie Defrin-September 25, 2025 Israel Defense Forces 25 Sep 2025
  • MFA Dir-Gen Bar Tal addresses international media Israel MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- Israel said yes to the Italian government's proposal to unload the aid at the port of Cyprus and then to transfer it to Gaza. The flotilla rejected the Italian proposal, proving that their real purpose is provocation and serving Hamas.
  • Prime Minister's Office Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 25 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the attack in Yemen from the Wing of Zion plane while en route to Washington, and received regular updates from the Defense Minister, IDF Chief-of-Staff and his Military Secretary afterward.

  • Secretary-General's remarks at the Ministerial Meeting in Support of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA) [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 25 Sep 2025
  • Foreign Minister IWAYA's attendance to the Ministerial Meeting in Support of United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 25 Sep 2025
  • Minister Anand meets with Commissioner-General of UN Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in Near East Canada 25 Sep 2025
  • Albares calls for building on current political momentum to achieve two-State solution Spain MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- The Minister advocates for "decisive action" to achieve the "just and comprehensive peace" that Israelis and Palestinians seek

  • Statement by UNRWA Commissioner-General Philippe Lazzarini at the Ministerial Meeting on UNRWA during High-Level Week at the 80th UNGA UNRWA 25 Sep 2025
  • Abbas rejects Hamas attack, urges world to recognise Palestinian statehood UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- Mahmoud Abbas, President of the State of Palestine, addressed the UN General Assembly on Thursday, declaring that his people were enduring a "war of genocide, destruction, starvation and displacement" at the hands of Israel's military in Gaza.
  • Gaza City suffering escalates as Israeli strikes inflict more heavy casualties UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- While the Israeli military's assault on Gaza City ramps up, conditions in the south of the war-torn enclave are so cramped that displaced and starving Gazans are sleeping on rubble-strewn open ground, UN aid teams said on Thursday.
  • Intervention of UNRWA Commissioner-General Philippe Lazzarini at the Meeting of the Global Alliance for the Implementation of the Two-State Solution UNRWA 25 Sep 2025

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Haiti UNOCHA 25 Sep 2025

  • Philippine side should stop provocative acts: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 25 Sep 2025 -- "The Philippine side should immediately stop any provocative acts before it's too late. The Chinese side will continue to take resolute measures to safeguard its territorial sovereignty and maritime rights and interests," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND), at a regular press conference on Thursday.
  • PBBM laments lack of progress in resolving South China Sea disputes Philippine News Agency 25 Sep 2025 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. on Thursday expressed dismay over the lack of further progress in addressing the longstanding South China Sea (SCS) disputes between the Philippines and China.

  • Prime Minister's Office announcement Israel - Prime Minister 25 Sep 2025 -- "Negotiations with Syria are taking place."

  • Libya's territorial integrity and sovereignty 'are non-negotiable' UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- The President of Libya's Presidential Council upheld the principle of national sovereignty, unity and stability in his speech to the UN General Assembly on Thursday.

  • Yemen calls for 'firm international action' to counter global threat posed by Houthis UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- Yemen's President Rashad Mohammed Al-Alimi on Thursday urged the international community to move beyond statements, to "firm international action" against Houthi separatists, saying the crisis provoked by the militants' international aggression now threatens regional and global security.

  • Joint Statement: Fourth Quadripartite Meeting of Foreign Ministers of China, Iran, Pakistan, and Russia, New York, 25 September 2025 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025 -- Upon the invitation of the Russian Federation, the fourth quadripartite Meeting of Foreign Ministers of the People's Republic of China, the Islamic Republic of Iran, the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, and the Russian Federation, on Afghanistan was held on 25 September 2025 on the sidelines of the 80th session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York.
    During the meeting, the four sides discussed the situation in Afghanistan and agreed on the following points:
    1- The four sides reiterated their support for Afghanistan as an independent, united, and peaceful state, free from terrorism, war, and narcotics.

  • OIC Secretary-General: The OIC IPHRC Emergency Meeting Sends a Unified Message, Strengthens the Legal and Diplomatic Positions of Member States, and Demonstrates Solidarity with the State of Qatar Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 25 Sep 2025

  • US Deputy Secretary of State Mr. Christopher Landau announced that, following Washington's recognition of Morocco's sovereignty over its Sahara, the US government has decided to encourage American investment in the Southern Provinces of the Kingdom. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025

  • Co-Chairs' Statement following the Ministerial Meeting on Coordinating Efforts for De-Escalation in Sudan UK FCDO 25 Sep 2025 -- Co-Chairs Statement from the African Union, the European Union, France, Germany and the United Kingdom following the Ministerial Meeting on Coordinating Efforts for De-Escalation in Sudan.

  • Haiti demands a new international force and reparations from France UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- "This is the face of Haiti today: a country at war, a modern-day Guernica, a human tragedy." From the podium of the UN General Assembly, Haiti's transitional leader delivered a stark plea to the international community.

  • Post-war Somalia proves multilateralism can make the world a better place, says President UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- In his address to the General Assembly, the President of Somalia Hassan Sheikh Mohamud outlined global challenges and shared the progress his country has made in the fight against terrorism, promoting inclusive governance and economic development.
  • Secretary-General's remarks at the High-level Meeting on Financing for the African Union Stabilization Mission in Somalia [as prepared for delivery] UN Secretary-General 25 Sep 2025

News Reports

  • Attorney General Bondi, Director Patel Statements Regarding Indictment of Former FBI Director James Comey US Dept. of Justice 25 Sep 2025 -- Today, a federal grand jury has charged former FBI Director James Comey with serious crimes related to the disclosure of sensitive information.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates of the Syrian Arab Republic Asaad al-Shaibani Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Meeting with IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi President of Russia 25 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin received Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Rafael Grossi in the Kremlin.
  • Meeting with Prime Minister of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed President of Russia 25 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin met with Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia Abiy Ahmed in the Kremlin.
  • Global Atomic Forum President of Russia 25 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin addressed the Global Atomic Forum taking place at the Atom Museum at VDNKh, Moscow.
  • Press release on Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's participation in the ministerial-level meeting of the United Nations Peacebuilding Commission Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's participation in a G20 ministerial meeting Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Statement by Sergey Lavrov at the G20 Foreign Ministers' Meeting on the sidelines of the 80th Session of the UN General Assembly, New York, September 25, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with President of the Plurinational State of Bolivia Luis Alberto Arce Catacora Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's meetings with UN Under-Secretary-General and Special Envoy for Digital and Emerging Technologies Amandeep Singh Gill and International Telecommunications Union Secretary-General Doreen Bogdan-Martin Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Cuba Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on September 25, 2025 PRC MofA 25 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Chinese Executive Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025.
  • Xi attends grand gathering in celebration of Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region's 70th founding anniversary PRC MND 25 Sep 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping attended on Thursday a grand gathering in celebration of the 70th founding anniversary of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region in Urumqi, the regional capital.
  • President Xi Jinping Delivers Video Remarks at the U.N. Climate Summit PRC MofA 25 Sep 2025 -- On September 24, 2025, President Xi Jinping delivered video remarks at the United Nations Climate Summit. 

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 25 Sep 2025

  • Maritime monitoring operations by the United Kingdom against illicit maritime activities including ship-to-ship transfers Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 25 Sep 2025
  • Targeting Illicit DPRK Weapons Sales to Burma and a DPRK Financial Facilitator US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025 -- Today, the United States sanctioned six targets for their roles in generating revenue for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), including by procuring materiel from DPRK for Burma's military regime, which has indiscriminately attacked civilian infrastructure since its 2021 coup.
  • Treasury Targets Arms Trafficking Network and Financial Facilitators for DPRK Weapons Programs US Treasury 25 Sep 2025 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is sanctioning five individuals and one entity for their role in generating illicit revenue for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) government's weapons of mass destruction (WMD) and ballistic missile programs, including by selling weapons to the Burmese military regime.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of the Republic of Iraq Fuad Hussein Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • The Reopening of the Iraq-Türkiye Pipeline US Dept. of State 25 Sep 2025 -- We welcome the announcement that the Government of Iraq has reached agreement with the Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) and international companies to reopen the Iraq-Türkiye pipeline, a deal facilitated by the United States that will bring tangible benefits for both Americans and Iraqis.

  • PM Netanyahu meets with President of Paraguay Santiago Peña in New York Israel MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- PM Netanyahu thanked President Peña for his unwavering support of Israel and his firm stance against antisemitism and against terror.
  • PM Netanyahu meets with President of Serbia Aleksandar Vučić Israel MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- PM Netanyahu expressed his gratitude to President Vučić for his unwavering support of Israel's efforts to free all of the hostages held by Hamas in Gaza,
  • PM Netanyahu meets with Argentine President Javier Milei in New York Israel MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- PM Netanyahu expressed his deep appreciation for President Milei who has stood steadfast against the systematic bias directed at Israel at the UN and in other international forums.

  • PM Netanyahu's Speech at the United Nations General Assembly Israel - Prime Minister 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy PM of Russia Dmitry Patrushev calls on Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi India MEA 25 Sep 2025

  • Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates, Mr. Nasser Bourita, held a bilateral meeting on Wednesday in New York with the UK Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, Mrs. Yvette Cooper. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025

  • Moldovan Oligarch Vladimir Plahotniuc Extradited, Days Before Pivotal Elections RFE/RL 25 Sep 2025 -- Moldovan oligarch Vladimir Plahotniuc has been extradited to Chisinau to face long-running criminal charges, jolting the country just days before a tense general election already roiled by allegations of Russian interference.

  • Addressing world challenges at the United Nations General Assembly Prime Minister of Australia 25 Sep 2025

  • Minister Anand meets with Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Canada 25 Sep 2025
  • Minister Anand meets with Haiti's Minister of Foreign Affairs Canada 25 Sep 2025
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Prime Minister of Jamaica and Chair of the Caribbean Community Andrew Holness Prime Minister of Canada 25 Sep 2025 -- Yesterday, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, met with the Prime Minister of Jamaica and Chair of the Caribbean Community (CARICOM), Andrew Holness, on the margins of the 80th Session of the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), in New York City, United States of America.

  • Meeting of the Foreign Ministers of the G4 Countries on UN Security Council Reform Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 25 Sep 2025
  • Japan-Cambodia Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 25 Sep 2025

  • Meeting with Acting President of Myanmar Min Aung Hlaing President of Russia 25 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting with Acting President of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar Min Aung Hlaing.
  • Press release on Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's meeting with UN Secretary-General's Special Envoy on Myanmar Julie Bishop Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025

  • Speech by President António Costa at the 80th United Nations General Assembly EC/CEU 25 Sep 2025

  • Secretary-General's remarks to meeting of G20 Foreign Ministers [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 25 Sep 2025
  • G20 Foreign Ministers' meeting: Remarks by High Representative/Vice-President Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 25 Sep 2025

  • Estonia imposes sanctions on two individuals undermining state sovereignty and territorial integrity Estonia MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna is imposing sanctions on two individuals who are undermining the sovereignty and territorial integrity of states and inciting separatism.
  • Government adopts new sanction against incitement to separatism Estonia MFA 25 Sep 2025 -- Today, 25 September, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna submitted to the government a proposal to adopt an additional Government of the Republic sanction against the undermining of sovereignty and territorial integrity, as well as the incitement of separatism.

  • Poland Reopens Belarus Border As China Moves To Protect Trade Routes RFE/RL 25 Sep 2025 -- Poland has reopened its border with Belarus after a nearly two-week closure tied to the Zapad-2025 joint military exercises held by Russia and Belarus, with Beijing playing a quiet but crucial role in the decision.
  • Deputy Prime Minister Radosław Sikorski visits New York Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025
  • MFA statement on entry ban for Irina Vlah Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Sep 2025

  • EU Monitoring Threats To Moldova's Elections Amid Alleged Russian Plot To Train Provocateurs In Serbia RFE/RL 25 Sep 2025 -- The European Commission is aware of Russia's attempts to influence Moldova's September 28 parliamentary elections and is examining "how it was possible" that dozens of Moldovans were reportedly trained in Serbia in destabilization tactics, Enlargement Commissioner Marta Kos told RFE/RL.

  • Speech by President António Costa at the UN Security Council on Artificial Intelligence and International Peace and Security EC/CEU 25 Sep 2025

  • UN moves to close dangerous void in AI governance UN News 25 Sep 2025 -- The explosive growth of AI tools around the world has yet to be matched by effective, internationally agreed rules on how this powerful technology is governed. A high-profile event at UN Headquarters on Thursday is designed to bring effective global AI governance a step closer to reality.
  • Secretary-General's remarks at High-level Multi-Stakeholder Informal Meeting to Launch the Global Dialogue on Artificial Intelligence Governance [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 25 Sep 2025
